object_under_evaluation: ordered stress recovery posture on one named owner surface and adjacent evidence family

handoff_fidelity.rationale: The handoff narrows behavior, blocks mutation, and carries evidence plus re-entry conditions.

route_discipline.rationale: Routing prefers reground-first and playbook-guided recovery over convenience re-entry.

reentry_quality.rationale: Re-entry is evidence-backed, but the window is still small and depends on one reviewed gate family.

regrounding_effectiveness.rationale: Projection health improves after regrounding, but success is tied to a narrow derived-surface family.

evidence_continuity.rationale: Owner receipts, handoffs, playbook gates, and route hints cite each other with little ambiguity.

false_promotion_prevention.rationale: Ticket creation and quarantine exit are not treated as health by themselves.

operator_burden.rationale: The operator still has to review regrounding and re-entry before trust widens.

trust_calibration.rationale: Degradation, quarantine, and re-entry limits stay visible throughout the window.

narrative_summary: The recovery window is healthier than a brittle fallback loop because the system stays explicit, bounded, and evidence-linked. The main weakness is that the window is still narrow and operator review remains important.
blind_spots: The report does not prove long-horizon health across unrelated stressor families.
The report does not prove that future KAG projection families will obey the same re-entry discipline.
evidence_gaps: Only one owner surface family is represented in this window.
There is limited repeated-window evidence for reduced operator burden.
